description: CPU-only EVAL COMPUTE-BUDGET gate: prices the e-0040..e-0047 statistically-recommended headline design (within-deep effective n=106 [>=2 frontier, e-0046], interior-k pass@k, multi-size DiD) as a MULTIPLE of the one eval run the project has PROVEN affordable -- e-0006/a-0005: 50 problems @ pass@1 = 50 samples in ~56 T4-min, reported within free-tier limits, => t1 = 1.12 T4-min/problem-sample. The whole power chain answered "is the effect resolvable?" and never the constraint CLAUDE.md makes first-class: "can we afford to resolve it?" total_samples = n*k*sizes*policies (pass@k needs k generations AND k Lean verifications per problem/model/policy); wall-clock bounded by LINEAR (verification-bound, conservative) and GEN-BATCHED (g=0.5, generation batches in k) regimes. Reads effective n + per-s_f best-k from e-0047's deep_interaction_passk_power.json. Pure stdlib; no Lean, no Modal. Prices EVAL only (excludes e-0007 QLoRA training); all figures PRE-SFT lower bounds (t1 from the degenerate 0.5B model whose junk Kimina rejects fast; SFT'd generation+verification is slower).
motivation: Connect the statistical recommendation to Modal cost discipline (CLAUDE.md): the interior-k lever e-0047 needs to make the interaction resolvable is the same multiplier that drives eval compute, and no gate priced the recommended design against what the project can afford.
formal_results:
Anchor: 50 samples / 56 T4-min => t1=1.120 min/problem-sample (PRE-SFT lower bound). Route: within-deep effective n=106, 2 policies. e-0047 powered best-k per weaker-policy rate s_f: {0.05:16, 0.10:16, 0.20:4}. Eval cost as MULTIPLES of the one proven-affordable run (=1.0x):
- full powered interaction (k=16, 3 sizes): 10176 samples = 204x = 190.0h T4 linear / 100.9h gen-batched.
- lean powered (k=8, 2 sizes): 3392 = 68x = 63.3h / 35.6h.
- cheap powered (k=4, 2 sizes): 1696 = 34x = 31.7h / 19.8h.
- single-size pass@1 fallback (k=1, 2 sizes): 424 = 8x = 7.9h.
- k=1 x 3 sizes: 636 = 13x = 11.9h. Cost is dominated by the irreducible n*k Lean verifications, so the interior-k lever e-0047 needs to RESOLVE the interaction is the SAME multiplier that drives the spend.
results_description: The statistically-recommended headline design is compute-EXPENSIVE relative to the only eval run shown to fit the free tier. Full powered interaction (k=16, 3 sizes) = 204x that run (190h T4 linear); even gen-batched it is 101h. The single-size pass@1 gap (q-0007 only, not the interaction) = 8x (7.9h), and the cheapest powered interaction (k=4, 2 sizes) = 34x.
conclusions: STAGE THE EVAL. Run the single-size pass@1 rough-vs-fine gap first (8x the proven run, resolves +0.11-0.16 per e-0046) and escalate to the multi-size interior-k interaction (68-204x) only once a single-size signal justifies it. The capstone interaction is statistically resolvable (e-0047) but its enabling lever -- a large interior k -- is exactly what makes it 1-2 orders of magnitude more expensive than the proven-affordable eval, before counting SFT training (e-0007) and the post-SFT slowdown both figures omit.
